title: The Lefty Call

jokes.text: Wow, that was quick.
jokes.setup: Larry goes to bathroom and comes out very quickly
jokes.punchline: Cha Cha commenting on his bathroom speed creates awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for bathroom monitoring theme. Economy is excellent - one line establishes the cringe dynamic.

jokes.text: You don't really need to comment on what's going on in terms of that or anything.
jokes.setup: Cha Cha's inappropriate bathroom commentary
jokes.punchline: Larry's polite but firm shutdown reveals his discomfort
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Larry trying to be polite while establishing boundaries. Sets up the entire episode's conflict.

jokes.text: You put your foot on the gas.
jokes.setup: Cha Cha asking how his hybrid car works
jokes.punchline: Larry gives the most obvious, unhelpful answer possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response. Maximum economy and structure - question begs for technical answer, gets obvious non-answer.

jokes.text: It was like having a bathroom monitor.
jokes.setup: Larry describing his bathroom experience with Cha Cha
jokes.punchline: Comparing adult workplace to elementary school dynamics
jokes.explanation: Excellent analogy that perfectly captures the absurdity. Strong character integration - only Larry would make this connection.

jokes.text: It's like going to the Port Authority or a whaling vessel.
jokes.setup: Describing rough recycled toilet paper
jokes.punchline: Escalating comparison from bad bathroom to maritime vessel
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from urban bathroom to 19th century maritime conditions. Unexpected and specific comparison that becomes a callback.

jokes.text: Is she supposed to use obscenities every other word, say 'fuck' and 'fuck this, cunt that' the whole time?
jokes.setup: Larry defending his criticism of Cha Cha
jokes.punchline: Saying the C-word in formal business lunch setting
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment - Larry saying the worst possible word in polite company. Character-driven shock value.

jokes.text: It's a little egotistical though, isn't it... to have so many of you? You want to bring so much more of you into the world.
jokes.setup: Couple mentions they have nine kids
jokes.punchline: Larry suggesting having many kids is narcissistic
jokes.explanation: Vintage Larry - taking an innocent comment and finding the most inappropriate angle. Sets up major plot consequence.

jokes.text: Why do you think they call it a goddamn doggy bag, huh?
jokes.setup: Waiter refusing to give doggy bag for actual dog
jokes.punchline: Larry pointing out the obvious etymology with frustration
jokes.explanation: Perfect logic trap - using the name to expose the absurdity of the policy. Excellent economy and structure.

jokes.text: Restaurant policy: 'We do not give food that was prepared for people to animals.'
jokes.setup: Larry wants to take leftovers home for his dog
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous specificity of the policy
jokes.explanation: Absurd bureaucratic logic that sets up the doggy bag escalation nicely.

jokes.text: We shall see.
jokes.setup: Larry promising to eat the food himself
jokes.punchline: Waiter's ominous response suggesting surveillance
jokes.explanation: Perfectly menacing line delivery. Maximum economy - two words create enormous comedic tension and plot setup.

jokes.text: Mm, that time again, huh?
jokes.setup: Larry heading to bathroom again
jokes.punchline: Cha Cha treating his bathroom needs like a scheduled appointment
jokes.explanation: Escalates the bathroom surveillance concept. Good character work showing Cha Cha's inappropriate familiarity.

jokes.text: Cha Cha offering Larry fruit: 'It's the ripest cantaloupe.' Larry: 'I've had fruit. Fruit's fruit.'
jokes.setup: Cha Cha enthusiastically offering fruit
jokes.punchline: Larry's dismissive generalization
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry dismissiveness - reducing all fruit to a generic category. Good character moment.

jokes.text: she's told me that you go to the bathroom, like, 10 to 14 times a day.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about Cha Cha's bathroom monitoring
jokes.punchline: Richard reveals she's been reporting specific numbers
jokes.explanation: Escalates the surveillance to absurd statistical tracking. Good structure revealing the extent of monitoring.

jokes.text: Someone's got to sit there. Would you rather have a klansman sitting there?
jokes.setup: Larry wanting a stranger as receptionist
jokes.punchline: Richard suggesting the alternative is a racist
jokes.explanation: Absurd false dichotomy typical of Richard Lewis character. Good shock value but slightly cheap escalation.

jokes.text: You drink more than a porpoise.
jokes.setup: Larry defending drinking lots of water
jokes.punchline: Comparing his water consumption to marine mammal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific comparison. Porpoise is unexpected and perfect - marine mammal known for water consumption.

jokes.text: I got a beautiful colon. You wanna take a picture of my colon?
jokes.setup: Richard questioning Larry's health
jokes.punchline: Larry defensively offering to show his colon
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry defensiveness - turning health concern into bizarre competitive offer. Great character integration.

jokes.text: I'll have a colon contest with you any time you want.
jokes.setup: Larry defending his colon health
jokes.punchline: Escalating to formal colon competition
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation to competitive medical examination. Perfect Larry character moment - defensive pride over intestinal health.

jokes.text: What are you eating? A lot of grains and fruit and nuts like a Jew squirrel?
jokes.setup: Discussing Larry's bathroom frequency
jokes.punchline: Combining Jewish stereotype with squirrel behavior
jokes.explanation: Unexpected combination of ethnic humor and animal comparison. Good character work for Richard Lewis.

jokes.text: I was trapped inside the stall
jokes.setup: Describing loud toilet flush
jokes.punchline: Being physically overwhelmed by toilet acoustics
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup. Slightly overwrought but creates good visual image.

jokes.text: You want me to talk lefty?
jokes.setup: Doctor suggesting using left ear for phone
jokes.punchline: Treating phone ear preference like handedness
jokes.explanation: Great concept - treating ear preference like throwing hand. Becomes central metaphor and episode title.

jokes.text: It's like throwing lefty. I can't do it. I'm not myself. I feel different, strange, like I'm not me.
jokes.setup: Explaining difficulty using left ear
jokes.punchline: Escalating to existential crisis over phone ear
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from minor inconvenience to identity crisis. Perfect Larry neurosis - earned through character.

jokes.text: This isn't McDonald's, okay?
jokes.setup: Larry asking to see another doctor immediately
jokes.punchline: Comparing medical office to fast food service
jokes.explanation: Solid straightman response. Good economy but fairly predictable comparison.

jokes.text: What the fuck are you looking at, Jewboy? Fucking faggot.
jokes.setup: Larry in doctor's waiting room
jokes.punchline: Racist confrontation sets up later plot
jokes.explanation: Setup more than punchline - establishes conflict for later payoff. Shock value without much craft.

jokes.text: Y'all got funny toilet paper here.
jokes.setup: Larry dealing with rough toilet paper complaints
jokes.punchline: Workers echoing his earlier Port Authority comparison
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure - other people validating Larry's earlier complaint. Earned through setup.

jokes.text: You pull their asshole open, step into their asshole, close the door behind you...
jokes.setup: Leon giving advice on handling confrontation
jokes.punchline: Elaborate metaphor about entering someone's rectum
jokes.explanation: Legendary Leon monologue. Completely original and character-specific. Extended metaphor is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: What are you wearing?
jokes.setup: Larry calling about movie plans
jokes.punchline: Accidentally making call sound sexual
jokes.explanation: Perfect accidental innuendo. Maximum economy - innocent question becomes inappropriate due to context.

jokes.text: What are you wearing? / You're making me uncomfortable, Larry.
jokes.setup: Larry and Cha Cha having a normal phone conversation
jokes.punchline: Larry's innocent question being misinterpreted as sexual harassment
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection showing how innocent questions become inappropriate in context. Great cringe structure.

jokes.text: anybody can play the bongos, come on. Do you have to be that skilled to bang on something?
jokes.setup: Talking about barbering looking easy
jokes.punchline: Comparing to bongos with unintentional double entendre
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo through 'banging' but not strongly developed. Average craft.

jokes.text: They do have nine kids.
jokes.setup: Jeff saying miscarriage is a shame
jokes.punchline: Larry suggesting it's not that bad given family size
jokes.explanation: Perfectly callous Larry response. Economy is excellent - few words, maximum inappropriate impact.

jokes.text: Larry's reaction to his quick haircut: 'You didn't take anything off.'
jokes.setup: Getting a very fast haircut
jokes.punchline: The obvious complaint stated matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Straightforward observational humor, competent but not particularly inspired.

jokes.text: We shall see.
jokes.setup: Discussing whether waiter poisoned the food
jokes.punchline: Repeating the waiter's ominous phrase
jokes.explanation: Strong callback - the phrase has become sinister. Good structure bringing it back at revelation moment.

jokes.text: Twice so far, two minutes each time.
jokes.setup: Waiter asking about Larry's bathroom frequency
jokes.punchline: Cha Cha providing precise bathroom statistics
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to bathroom surveillance plot. Specific timing data makes the monitoring even more invasive.

jokes.text: You liars, you were complaining about the toilet paper.
jokes.setup: Larry caught with soft toilet paper
jokes.punchline: Cheryl exposing the toilet paper conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Good revelation moment - Cheryl catching everyone in the scheme. Solid structure for plot resolution.

jokes.text: Those people would be killed in China.
jokes.setup: Arguing about having too many kids
jokes.punchline: Invoking Chinese population control as moral authority
jokes.explanation: Good Larry logic - using authoritarian policy as moral benchmark. Character-appropriate absurdist argument.

jokes.text: I want a righty call.
jokes.setup: Larry forced to use left ear by doctor
jokes.punchline: Demanding to switch back to right ear
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to episode title concept. Good physical comedy as Larry struggles with phone.

jokes.text: You get in that ass.
jokes.setup: Spotting the supposed skinhead
jokes.punchline: Leon ready to implement his asshole strategy
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - Leon ready to execute his bizarre strategy. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Come on, you fucking skinhead, you piece of shit, let's go! / He is not a skinhead. He just came out of chemotherapy. / Sorry.
jokes.setup: Larry finally confronting the 'skinhead' from earlier
jokes.punchline: Revelation that the man is a cancer patient, not a bigot
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection revealing Larry's assumption was completely wrong. Perfect cringe culmination of Leon's advice.

jokes.text: He is not a skinhead. He just came out of chemotherapy.
jokes.setup: Larry calling patient a skinhead
jokes.punchline: Revealing the man is actually a cancer patient
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - perfect setup and devastating reveal. Excellent structure and maximum cringe impact.

tmdb_overview: Larry regrets getting Cha-Cha a job in his building when she monitors his bathroom habits, and runs into further problems with her when he has to make phone calls 'lefty'; the Blacks and Larry are fed up with Cheryl's environmentally conscious, but uncomfortable, toilet paper; a waiter objects to a doggie bag actually being prepared for a dog.
